endpoints.name: Get attachments from an object
endpoints.description:
Returns the compact records for all attachments on the object.

There are three possible `parent` values for this request: `project`, `project_brief`, and `task`. For a project, an attachment refers to a file uploaded to the "Key resources" section in the project Overview. For a project brief, an attachment refers to inline files in the project brief itself. For a task, an attachment refers to a file directly associated to that task.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Upload an attachment
endpoints.description:
Upload an attachment.

This method uploads an attachment on an object and returns the compact
record for the created attachment object. This is possible by either:

- Providing the URL of the external resource being attached, or
- Downloading the file content first and then uploading it as any other attachment. Note that it is not possible to attach
files from third party services such as Dropbox, Box, Vimeo & Google Drive via the API

The 100MB size limit on attachments in Asana is enforced on this endpoint.

This endpoint expects a multipart/form-data encoded request containing the full contents of the file to be uploaded.

Requests made should follow the HTTP/1.1 specification that line
terminators are of the form `CRLF` or `\r\n` outlined
[here](http://www.w3.org/Protocols/HTTP/1.1/draft-ietf-http-v11-spec-01#Basic-Rules) in order for the server to reliably and properly handle the request.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get an attachment
endpoints.description: Get the full record for a single attachment.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{ATTACHMENT_GID}} with a real attachment_gid.

endpoints.name: Delete an attachment
endpoints.description:
Deletes a specific, existing attachment.

Returns an empty data record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{ATTACHMENT_GID}} with a real attachment_gid.

endpoints.name: Submit parallel requests
endpoints.description: Make multiple requests in parallel to Asana's API.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Create a custom field
endpoints.description:
Creates a new custom field in a workspace. Every custom field is required
to be created in a specific workspace, and this workspace cannot be
changed once set.

A custom field’s name must be unique within a workspace and not conflict
with names of existing task properties such as `Due Date` or `Assignee`.
A custom field’s type must be one of `text`, `enum`, `multi_enum`, `number`,
`date`, or `people`.

Returns the full record of the newly created custom field.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a custom field
endpoints.description:
Get the complete definition of a custom field’s metadata.

Since custom fields can be defined for one of a number of types, and
these types have different data and behaviors, there are fields that are
relevant to a particular type. For instance, as noted above, enum_options
is only relevant for the enum type and defines the set of choices that
the enum could represent. The examples below show some of these
type-specific custom field definitions.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{CUSTOM_FIELD_GID}} with a real custom_field_gid.

endpoints.name: Update a custom field
endpoints.description:
A specific, existing custom field can be updated by making a PUT request on the URL for that custom field. Only the fields provided in the `data` block will be updated; any unspecified fields will remain unchanged
When using this method, it is best to specify only those fields you wish to change, or else you may overwrite changes made by another user since you last retrieved the custom field.
A custom field’s `type` cannot be updated.
An enum custom field’s `enum_options` cannot be updated with this endpoint. Instead see “Work With Enum Options” for information on how to update `enum_options`.
Locked custom fields can only be updated by the user who locked the field.
Returns the complete updated custom field record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{CUSTOM_FIELD_GID}} with a real custom_field_gid.

endpoints.name: Delete a custom field
endpoints.description:
A specific, existing custom field can be deleted by making a DELETE request on the URL for that custom field.
Locked custom fields can only be deleted by the user who locked the field.
Returns an empty data record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{CUSTOM_FIELD_GID}} with a real custom_field_gid.

endpoints.name: Create an enum option
endpoints.description:
Creates an enum option and adds it to this custom field’s list of enum options. A custom field can have at most 500 enum options (including disabled options). By default new enum options are inserted at the end of a custom field’s list.
Locked custom fields can only have enum options added by the user who locked the field.
Returns the full record of the newly created enum option.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{CUSTOM_FIELD_GID}} with a real custom_field_gid.

endpoints.name: Reorder a custom field's enum
endpoints.description:
Moves a particular enum option to be either before or after another specified enum option in the custom field.
Locked custom fields can only be reordered by the user who locked the field.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{CUSTOM_FIELD_GID}} with a real custom_field_gid.

endpoints.name: Update an enum option
endpoints.description:
Updates an existing enum option. Enum custom fields require at least one enabled enum option.
Locked custom fields can only be updated by the user who locked the field.
Returns the full record of the updated enum option.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{ENUM_OPTION_GID}} with a real enum_option_gid.

endpoints.name: Get events on a resource
endpoints.description:
Returns the full record for all events that have occurred since the sync
token was created.

A `GET` request to the endpoint `/[path_to_resource]/events` can be made in
lieu of including the resource ID in the data for the request.

Asana limits a single sync token to 100 events. If more than 100 events exist
for a given resource, `has_more: true` will be returned in the response, indicating
that there are more events to pull. 

*Note: The resource returned will be the resource that triggered the
event. This may be different from the one that the events were requested
for. For example, a subscription to a project will contain events for
tasks contained within the project.*
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get goal relationships
endpoints.description: Returns compact goal relationship records.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a goal relationship
endpoints.description: Returns the complete updated goal relationship record for a single goal relationship.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{GOAL_RELATIONSHIP_GID}} with a real goal_relationship_gid.

endpoints.name: Update a goal relationship
endpoints.description:
An existing goal relationship can be updated by making a PUT request on the URL for
that goal relationship. Only the fields provided in the `data` block will be updated;
any unspecified fields will remain unchanged.

Returns the complete updated goal relationship record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{GOAL_RELATIONSHIP_GID}} with a real goal_relationship_gid.

endpoints.name: Get goals
endpoints.description: Returns compact goal records.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Create a goal
endpoints.description:
Creates a new goal in a workspace or team.

Returns the full record of the newly created goal.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a goal
endpoints.description: Returns the complete goal record for a single goal.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{GOAL_GID}} with a real goal_gid.

endpoints.name: Update a goal
endpoints.description:
An existing goal can be updated by making a PUT request on the URL for
that goal. Only the fields provided in the `data` block will be updated;
any unspecified fields will remain unchanged.

Returns the complete updated goal record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{GOAL_GID}} with a real goal_gid.

endpoints.name: Delete a goal
endpoints.description:
A specific, existing goal can be deleted by making a DELETE request on the URL for that goal.

Returns an empty data record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{GOAL_GID}} with a real goal_gid.

endpoints.name: Add a collaborator to a goal
endpoints.description:
Adds followers to a goal. Returns the goal the followers were added to.
Each goal can be associated with zero or more followers in the system.
Requests to add/remove followers, if successful, will return the complete updated goal record, described above.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{GOAL_GID}} with a real goal_gid.

endpoints.name: Add a supporting goal relationship
endpoints.description:
Creates a goal relationship by adding a supporting resource to a given goal.

Returns the newly created goal relationship record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{GOAL_GID}} with a real goal_gid.

endpoints.name: Get parent goals from a goal
endpoints.description: Returns a compact representation of all of the parent goals of a goal.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{GOAL_GID}} with a real goal_gid.

endpoints.name: Remove a collaborator from a goal
endpoints.description:
Removes followers from a goal. Returns the goal the followers were removed from.
Each goal can be associated with zero or more followers in the system.
Requests to add/remove followers, if successful, will return the complete updated goal record, described above.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{GOAL_GID}} with a real goal_gid.

endpoints.name: Removes a supporting goal relationship
endpoints.description: Removes a goal relationship for a given parent goal.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{GOAL_GID}} with a real goal_gid.

endpoints.name: Create a goal metric
endpoints.description: Creates and adds a goal metric to a specified goal. Note that this replaces an existing goal metric if one already exists.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{GOAL_GID}} with a real goal_gid.

endpoints.name: Update a goal metric
endpoints.description:
Updates a goal's existing metric's `current_number_value` if one exists,
otherwise responds with a 400 status code.

Returns the complete updated goal metric record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{GOAL_GID}} with a real goal_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a job by id
endpoints.description: Returns the full record for a job.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{JOB_GID}} with a real job_gid.

endpoints.name: Create an organization export request
endpoints.description: This method creates a request to export an Organization. Asana will complete the export at some point after you create the request.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get details on an org export request
endpoints.description: Returns details of a previously-requested Organization export.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{ORGANIZATION_EXPORT_GID}} with a real organization_export_gid.

endpoints.name: Get multiple portfolio memberships
endpoints.description: Returns a list of portfolio memberships in compact representation. You must specify `portfolio`, `portfolio` and `user`, or `workspace` and `user`.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a portfolio membership
endpoints.description: Returns the complete portfolio record for a single portfolio membership.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PORTFOLIO_MEMBERSHIP_GID}} with a real portfolio_membership_gid.

endpoints.name: Get multiple portfolios
endpoints.description: Returns a list of the portfolios in compact representation that are owned by the current API user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Create a portfolio
endpoints.description:
Creates a new portfolio in the given workspace with the supplied name.

Note that portfolios created in the Asana UI may have some state
(like the “Priority” custom field) which is automatically added
to the portfolio when it is created. Portfolios created via our
API will *not* be created with the same initial state to allow
integrations to create their own starting state on a portfolio.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a portfolio
endpoints.description: Returns the complete portfolio record for a single portfolio.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PORTFOLIO_GID}} with a real portfolio_gid.

endpoints.name: Update a portfolio
endpoints.description:
An existing portfolio can be updated by making a PUT request on the URL for
that portfolio. Only the fields provided in the `data` block will be updated;
any unspecified fields will remain unchanged.

Returns the complete updated portfolio record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PORTFOLIO_GID}} with a real portfolio_gid.

endpoints.name: Delete a portfolio
endpoints.description:
An existing portfolio can be deleted by making a DELETE request on
the URL for that portfolio.

Returns an empty data record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PORTFOLIO_GID}} with a real portfolio_gid.

endpoints.name: Add a custom field to a portfolio
endpoints.description: Custom fields are associated with portfolios by way of custom field settings.  This method creates a setting for the portfolio.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PORTFOLIO_GID}} with a real portfolio_gid.

endpoints.name: Add a portfolio item
endpoints.description:
Add an item to a portfolio.
Returns an empty data block.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PORTFOLIO_GID}} with a real portfolio_gid.

endpoints.name: Add users to a portfolio
endpoints.description:
Adds the specified list of users as members of the portfolio.
Returns the updated portfolio record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PORTFOLIO_GID}} with a real portfolio_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a portfolio's custom fields
endpoints.description: Returns a list of all of the custom fields settings on a portfolio, in compact form.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PORTFOLIO_GID}} with a real portfolio_gid.

endpoints.name: Get portfolio items
endpoints.description: Get a list of the items in compact form in a portfolio.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PORTFOLIO_GID}} with a real portfolio_gid.

endpoints.name: Get memberships from a portfolio
endpoints.description: Returns the compact portfolio membership records for the portfolio.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PORTFOLIO_GID}} with a real portfolio_gid.

endpoints.name: Remove a custom field from a portfolio
endpoints.description: Removes a custom field setting from a portfolio.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PORTFOLIO_GID}} with a real portfolio_gid.

endpoints.name: Remove a portfolio item
endpoints.description:
Remove an item from a portfolio.
Returns an empty data block.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PORTFOLIO_GID}} with a real portfolio_gid.

endpoints.name: Remove users from a portfolio
endpoints.description:
Removes the specified list of users from members of the portfolio.
Returns the updated portfolio record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PORTFOLIO_GID}} with a real portfolio_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a project brief
endpoints.description: Get the full record for a project brief.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_BRIEF_GID}} with a real project_brief_gid.

endpoints.name: Update a project brief
endpoints.description:
An existing project brief can be updated by making a PUT request on the URL for
that project brief. Only the fields provided in the `data` block will be updated;
any unspecified fields will remain unchanged.

Returns the complete updated project brief record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_BRIEF_GID}} with a real project_brief_gid.

endpoints.name: Delete a project brief
endpoints.description:
Deletes a specific, existing project brief.

Returns an empty data record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_BRIEF_GID}} with a real project_brief_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a project membership
endpoints.description: Returns the complete project record for a single project membership.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_MEMBERSHIP_GID}} with a real project_membership_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a project status
endpoints.description:
*Deprecated: new integrations should prefer the `/status_updates/{status_gid}` route.*

Returns the complete record for a single status update.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_STATUS_GID}} with a real project_status_gid.

endpoints.name: Delete a project status
endpoints.description:
*Deprecated: new integrations should prefer the `/status_updates/{status_gid}` route.*

Deletes a specific, existing project status update.

Returns an empty data record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_STATUS_GID}} with a real project_status_gid.

endpoints.name: Get multiple project templates
endpoints.description: Returns the compact project template records for all project templates in the given team or workspace.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a project template
endpoints.description: Returns the complete project template record for a single project template.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_TEMPLATE_GID}} with a real project_template_gid.

endpoints.name: Instantiate a project from a project template
endpoints.description:
Creates and returns a job that will asynchronously handle the project instantiation.

To form this request, it is recommended to first make a request to [get a project template](/docs/get-a-project-template). Then, from the response, copy the `gid` from the object in the `requested_dates` array. This `gid` should be used in `requested_dates` to instantiate a project.

_Note: The body of this request will differ if your workspace is an organization. To determine if your workspace is an organization, use the [is_organization](/docs/workspace) parameter._
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_TEMPLATE_GID}} with a real project_template_gid.

endpoints.name: Get multiple projects
endpoints.description:
Returns the compact project records for some filtered set of projects. Use one or more of the parameters provided to filter the projects returned.
*Note: This endpoint may timeout for large domains. Try filtering by team!*
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Create a project
endpoints.description:
Create a new project in a workspace or team.

Every project is required to be created in a specific workspace or
organization, and this cannot be changed once set. Note that you can use
the `workspace` parameter regardless of whether or not it is an
organization.

If the workspace for your project is an organization, you must also
supply a `team` to share the project with.

Returns the full record of the newly created project.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a project
endpoints.description: Returns the complete project record for a single project.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Update a project
endpoints.description:
A specific, existing project can be updated by making a PUT request on
the URL for that project. Only the fields provided in the `data` block
will be updated; any unspecified fields will remain unchanged.

When using this method, it is best to specify only those fields you wish
to change, or else you may overwrite changes made by another user since
you last retrieved the task.

Returns the complete updated project record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Delete a project
endpoints.description:
A specific, existing project can be deleted by making a DELETE request on
the URL for that project.

Returns an empty data record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Add a custom field to a project
endpoints.description: Custom fields are associated with projects by way of custom field settings.  This method creates a setting for the project.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Add followers to a project
endpoints.description:
Adds the specified list of users as followers to the project. Followers are a subset of members who have opted in to receive "tasks added" notifications for a project. Therefore, if the users are not already members of the project, they will also become members as a result of this operation.
Returns the updated project record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Add users to a project
endpoints.description:
Adds the specified list of users as members of the project. Note that a user being added as a member may also be added as a *follower* as a result of this operation. This is because the user's default notification settings (i.e., in the "Notifcations" tab of "My Profile Settings") will override this endpoint's default behavior of setting "Tasks added" notifications to `false`.
Returns the updated project record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a project's custom fields
endpoints.description: Returns a list of all of the custom fields settings on a project, in compact form. Note that, as in all queries to collections which return compact representation, `opt_fields` can be used to include more data than is returned in the compact representation. See the [getting started guide on input/output options](https://developers.asana.com/docs/#input-output-options) for more information.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Duplicate a project
endpoints.description: Creates and returns a job that will asynchronously handle the duplication.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Create a project brief
endpoints.description:
Creates a new project brief.

Returns the full record of the newly created project brief.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Get memberships from a project
endpoints.description: Returns the compact project membership records for the project.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Get statuses from a project
endpoints.description:
*Deprecated: new integrations should prefer the `/status_updates` route.*

Returns the compact project status update records for all updates on the project.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Create a project status
endpoints.description:
*Deprecated: new integrations should prefer the `/status_updates` route.*

Creates a new status update on the project.

Returns the full record of the newly created project status update.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Remove a custom field from a project
endpoints.description: Removes a custom field setting from a project.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Remove followers from a project
endpoints.description:
Removes the specified list of users from following the project, this will not affect project membership status.
Returns the updated project record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Remove users from a project
endpoints.description:
Removes the specified list of users from members of the project.
Returns the updated project record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Create a project template from a project
endpoints.description:
Creates and returns a job that will asynchronously handle the project template creation. Note that
while the resulting project template can be accessed with the API, it won't be visible in the Asana
UI until Project Templates 2.0 is launched in the app. See more in [this forum post](https://forum.asana.com/t/a-new-api-for-project-templates/156432).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Get sections in a project
endpoints.description: Returns the compact records for all sections in the specified project.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Create a section in a project
endpoints.description:
Creates a new section in a project.
Returns the full record of the newly created section.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Move or Insert sections
endpoints.description:
Move sections relative to each other. One of
`before_section` or `after_section` is required.

Sections cannot be moved between projects.

Returns an empty data block.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Get task count of a project
endpoints.description:
Get an object that holds task count fields. **All fields are excluded by default**. You must [opt in](/docs/input-output-options) using `opt_fields` to get any information from this endpoint.

This endpoint has an additional [rate limit](/docs/standard-rate-limits) and each field counts especially high against our [cost limits](/docs/cost-limits).

Milestones are just tasks, so they are included in the `num_tasks`, `num_incomplete_tasks`, and `num_completed_tasks` counts.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Get tasks from a project
endpoints.description: Returns the compact task records for all tasks within the given project, ordered by their priority within the project. Tasks can exist in more than one project at a time.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{PROJECT_GID}} with a real project_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a section
endpoints.description: Returns the complete record for a single section.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{SECTION_GID}} with a real section_gid.

endpoints.name: Update a section
endpoints.description:
A specific, existing section can be updated by making a PUT request on
the URL for that project. Only the fields provided in the `data` block
will be updated; any unspecified fields will remain unchanged. (note that
at this time, the only field that can be updated is the `name` field.)

When using this method, it is best to specify only those fields you wish
to change, or else you may overwrite changes made by another user since
you last retrieved the task.

Returns the complete updated section record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{SECTION_GID}} with a real section_gid.

endpoints.name: Delete a section
endpoints.description:
A specific, existing section can be deleted by making a DELETE request on
the URL for that section.

Note that sections must be empty to be deleted.

The last remaining section cannot be deleted.

Returns an empty data block.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{SECTION_GID}} with a real section_gid.

endpoints.name: Add task to section
endpoints.description:
Add a task to a specific, existing section. This will remove the task from other sections of the project.

The task will be inserted at the top of a section unless an insert_before or insert_after parameter is declared.

This does not work for separators (tasks with the resource_subtype of section).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{SECTION_GID}} with a real section_gid.

endpoints.name: Get tasks from a section
endpoints.description: *Board view only*: Returns the compact section records for all tasks within the given section.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{SECTION_GID}} with a real section_gid.

endpoints.name: Get status updates from an object
endpoints.description: Returns the compact status update records for all updates on the object.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Create a status update
endpoints.description:
Creates a new status update on an object.
Returns the full record of the newly created status update.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a status update
endpoints.description: Returns the complete record for a single status update.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{STATUS_GID}} with a real status_gid.

endpoints.name: Delete a status update
endpoints.description:
Deletes a specific, existing status update.

Returns an empty data record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{STATUS_GID}} with a real status_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a story
endpoints.description: Returns the full record for a single story.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{STORY_GID}} with a real story_gid.

endpoints.name: Update a story
endpoints.description: Updates the story and returns the full record for the updated story. Only comment stories can have their text updated, and only comment stories and attachment stories can be pinned. Only one of `text` and `html_text` can be specified.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{STORY_GID}} with a real story_gid.

endpoints.name: Delete a story
endpoints.description:
Deletes a story. A user can only delete stories they have created.

Returns an empty data record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{STORY_GID}} with a real story_gid.

endpoints.name: Get multiple tags
endpoints.description: Returns the compact tag records for some filtered set of tags. Use one or more of the parameters provided to filter the tags returned.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Create a tag
endpoints.description:
Creates a new tag in a workspace or organization.

Every tag is required to be created in a specific workspace or
organization, and this cannot be changed once set. Note that you can use
the workspace parameter regardless of whether or not it is an
organization.

Returns the full record of the newly created tag.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a tag
endpoints.description: Returns the complete tag record for a single tag.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TAG_GID}} with a real tag_gid.

endpoints.name: Update a tag
endpoints.description:
Updates the properties of a tag. Only the fields provided in the `data`
block will be updated; any unspecified fields will remain unchanged.

When using this method, it is best to specify only those fields you wish
to change, or else you may overwrite changes made by another user since
you last retrieved the tag.

Returns the complete updated tag record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TAG_GID}} with a real tag_gid.

endpoints.name: Delete a tag
endpoints.description:
A specific, existing tag can be deleted by making a DELETE request on
the URL for that tag.

Returns an empty data record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TAG_GID}} with a real tag_gid.

endpoints.name: Get tasks from a tag
endpoints.description: Returns the compact task records for all tasks with the given tag. Tasks can have more than one tag at a time.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TAG_GID}} with a real tag_gid.

endpoints.name: Get multiple tasks
endpoints.description:
Returns the compact task records for some filtered set of tasks. Use one or more of the parameters provided to filter the tasks returned. You must specify a `project` or `tag` if you do not specify `assignee` and `workspace`.

For more complex task retrieval, use [workspaces/{workspace_gid}/tasks/search](/docs/search-tasks-in-a-workspace).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Create a task
endpoints.description:
Creating a new task is as easy as POSTing to the `/tasks` endpoint with a
data block containing the fields you’d like to set on the task. Any
unspecified fields will take on default values.

Every task is required to be created in a specific workspace, and this
workspace cannot be changed once set. The workspace need not be set
explicitly if you specify `projects` or a `parent` task instead.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a task
endpoints.description: Returns the complete task record for a single task.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Update a task
endpoints.description:
A specific, existing task can be updated by making a PUT request on the
URL for that task. Only the fields provided in the `data` block will be
updated; any unspecified fields will remain unchanged.

When using this method, it is best to specify only those fields you wish
to change, or else you may overwrite changes made by another user since
you last retrieved the task.

Returns the complete updated task record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Delete a task
endpoints.description:
A specific, existing task can be deleted by making a DELETE request on
the URL for that task. Deleted tasks go into the “trash” of the user
making the delete request. Tasks can be recovered from the trash within a
period of 30 days; afterward they are completely removed from the system.

Returns an empty data record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Set dependencies for a task
endpoints.description: Marks a set of tasks as dependencies of this task, if they are not already dependencies. *A task can have at most 30 dependents and dependencies combined*.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Set dependents for a task
endpoints.description: Marks a set of tasks as dependents of this task, if they are not already dependents. *A task can have at most 30 dependents and dependencies combined*.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Add followers to a task
endpoints.description:
Adds followers to a task. Returns an empty data block.
Each task can be associated with zero or more followers in the system.
Requests to add/remove followers, if successful, will return the complete updated task record, described above.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Add a project to a task
endpoints.description:
Adds the task to the specified project, in the optional location
specified. If no location arguments are given, the task will be added to
the end of the project.

`addProject` can also be used to reorder a task within a project or
section that already contains it.

At most one of `insert_before`, `insert_after`, or `section` should be
specified. Inserting into a section in an non-order-dependent way can be
done by specifying section, otherwise, to insert within a section in a
particular place, specify `insert_before` or `insert_after` and a task
within the section to anchor the position of this task.

Returns an empty data block.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Add a tag to a task
endpoints.description: Adds a tag to a task. Returns an empty data block.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Get dependencies from a task
endpoints.description: Returns the compact representations of all of the dependencies of a task.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Get dependents from a task
endpoints.description: Returns the compact representations of all of the dependents of a task.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Duplicate a task
endpoints.description: Creates and returns a job that will asynchronously handle the duplication.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Get projects a task is in
endpoints.description: Returns a compact representation of all of the projects the task is in.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Unlink dependencies from a task
endpoints.description: Unlinks a set of dependencies from this task.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Unlink dependents from a task
endpoints.description: Unlinks a set of dependents from this task.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Remove followers from a task
endpoints.description: Removes each of the specified followers from the task if they are following. Returns the complete, updated record for the affected task.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Remove a project from a task
endpoints.description:
Removes the task from the specified project. The task will still exist in
the system, but it will not be in the project anymore.

Returns an empty data block.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Remove a tag from a task
endpoints.description: Removes a tag from a task. Returns an empty data block.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Set the parent of a task
endpoints.description: parent, or no parent task at all. Returns an empty data block. When using `insert_before` and `insert_after`, at most one of those two options can be specified, and they must already be subtasks of the parent.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Get stories from a task
endpoints.description: Returns the compact records for all stories on the task.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Create a story on a task
endpoints.description:
Adds a story to a task. This endpoint currently only allows for comment
stories to be created. The comment will be authored by the currently
authenticated user, and timestamped when the server receives the request.

Returns the full record for the new story added to the task.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Get subtasks from a task
endpoints.description: Returns a compact representation of all of the subtasks of a task.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Create a subtask
endpoints.description: Creates a new subtask and adds it to the parent task. Returns the full record for the newly created subtask.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a task's tags
endpoints.description: Get a compact representation of all of the tags the task has.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TASK_GID}} with a real task_gid.

endpoints.name: Get team memberships
endpoints.description: Returns compact team membership records.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a team membership
endpoints.description: Returns the complete team membership record for a single team membership.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TEAM_MEMBERSHIP_GID}} with a real team_membership_gid.

endpoints.name: Create a team
endpoints.description: Creates a team within the current workspace.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Update a team
endpoints.description: Updates a team within the current workspace.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a team
endpoints.description: Returns the full record for a single team.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TEAM_GID}} with a real team_gid.

endpoints.name: Add a user to a team
endpoints.description:
The user making this call must be a member of the team in order to add others. The user being added must exist in the same organization as the team.

Returns the complete team membership record for the newly added user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TEAM_GID}} with a real team_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a team's project templates
endpoints.description: Returns the compact project template records for all project templates in the team.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TEAM_GID}} with a real team_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a team's projects
endpoints.description: Returns the compact project records for all projects in the team.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TEAM_GID}} with a real team_gid.

endpoints.name: Create a project in a team
endpoints.description:
Creates a project shared with the given team.

Returns the full record of the newly created project.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TEAM_GID}} with a real team_gid.

endpoints.name: Remove a user from a team
endpoints.description: The user making this call must be a member of the team in order to remove themselves or others.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TEAM_GID}} with a real team_gid.

endpoints.name: Get memberships from a team
endpoints.description: Returns the compact team memberships for the team.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TEAM_GID}} with a real team_gid.

endpoints.name: Get users in a team
endpoints.description:
Returns the compact records for all users that are members of the team.
Results are sorted alphabetically and limited to 2000. For more results use the `/users` endpoint.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TEAM_GID}} with a real team_gid.

endpoints.name: Get time periods
endpoints.description: Returns compact time period records.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a time period
endpoints.description: Returns the full record for a single time period.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{TIME_PERIOD_GID}} with a real time_period_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a user task list
endpoints.description: Returns the full record for a user task list.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{USER_TASK_LIST_GID}} with a real user_task_list_gid.

endpoints.name: Get tasks from a user task list
endpoints.description:
Returns the compact list of tasks in a user’s My Tasks list.
*Note: Access control is enforced for this endpoint as with all Asana API endpoints, meaning a user’s private tasks will be filtered out if the API-authenticated user does not have access to them.*
*Note: Both complete and incomplete tasks are returned by default unless they are filtered out (for example, setting `completed_since=now` will return only incomplete tasks, which is the default view for “My Tasks” in Asana.)*
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{USER_TASK_LIST_GID}} with a real user_task_list_gid.

endpoints.name: Get multiple users
endpoints.description:
Returns the user records for all users in all workspaces and organizations accessible to the authenticated user. Accepts an optional workspace ID parameter.
Results are sorted by user ID.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a user
endpoints.description: Returns the full user record for the single user with the provided ID.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{USER_GID}} with a real user_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a user's favorites
endpoints.description:
Returns all of a user's favorites in the given workspace, of the given type.
Results are given in order (The same order as Asana's sidebar).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{USER_GID}} with a real user_gid.

endpoints.name: Get memberships from a user
endpoints.description: Returns the compact team membership records for the user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{USER_GID}} with a real user_gid.

endpoints.name: Get teams for a user
endpoints.description: Returns the compact records for all teams to which the given user is assigned.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{USER_GID}} with a real user_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a user's task list
endpoints.description: Returns the full record for a user's task list.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{USER_GID}} with a real user_gid.

endpoints.name: Get workspace memberships for a user
endpoints.description: Returns the compact workspace membership records for the user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{USER_GID}} with a real user_gid.

endpoints.name: Get multiple webhooks
endpoints.description: Get the compact representation of all webhooks your app has registered for the authenticated user in the given workspace.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a webhook
endpoints.description: Returns the full record for the given webhook.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WEBHOOK_GID}} with a real webhook_gid.

endpoints.name: Update a webhook
endpoints.description: An existing webhook's filters can be updated by making a PUT request on the URL for that webhook. Note that the webhook's previous `filters` array will be completely overwritten by the `filters` sent in the PUT request.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WEBHOOK_GID}} with a real webhook_gid.

endpoints.name: Delete a webhook
endpoints.description: This method *permanently* removes a webhook. Note that it may be possible to receive a request that was already in flight after deleting the webhook, but no further requests will be issued.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WEBHOOK_GID}} with a real webhook_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a workspace membership
endpoints.description: Returns the complete workspace record for a single workspace membership.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_MEMBERSHIP_GID}} with a real workspace_membership_gid.

endpoints.name: Get multiple workspaces
endpoints.description: Returns the compact records for all workspaces visible to the authorized user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get a workspace
endpoints.description: Returns the full workspace record for a single workspace.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.

endpoints.name: Update a workspace
endpoints.description:
A specific, existing workspace can be updated by making a PUT request on the URL for that workspace. Only the fields provided in the data block will be updated; any unspecified fields will remain unchanged.
Currently the only field that can be modified for a workspace is its name.
Returns the complete, updated workspace record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.

endpoints.name: Add a user to a workspace or organization
endpoints.description:
Add a user to a workspace or organization.
The user can be referenced by their globally unique user ID or their email address. Returns the full user record for the invited user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.

endpoints.name: Get audit log events
endpoints.description:
Retrieve the audit log events that have been captured in your domain.

This endpoint will return a list of [AuditLogEvent](/docs/audit-log-event) objects, sorted by creation time in ascending order. Note that the Audit Log API captures events from October 8th, 2021 and later. Queries for events before this date will not return results.

There are a number of query parameters (below) that can be used to filter the set of [AuditLogEvent](/docs/audit-log-event) objects that are returned in the response. Any combination of query parameters is valid. When no filters are provided, all of the events that have been captured in your domain will match.

The list of events will always be [paginated](/docs/pagination). The default limit is 1000 events. The next set of events can be retrieved using the `offset` from the previous response. If there are no events that match the provided filters in your domain, the endpoint will return `null` for the `next_page` field. Querying again with the same filters may return new events if they were captured after the last request. Once a response includes a `next_page` with an `offset`, subsequent requests can be made with the latest `offset` to poll for new events that match the provided filters.

When no `offset` is provided, the response will begin with the oldest events that match the provided filters. It is important to note that [AuditLogEvent](/docs/audit-log-event) objects will be permanently deleted from our systems after 90 days. If you wish to keep a permanent record of these events, we recommend using a SIEM tool to ingest and store these logs.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.

endpoints.name: Get a workspace's custom fields
endpoints.description: Returns a list of the compact representation of all of the custom fields in a workspace.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.

endpoints.name: Get all projects in a workspace
endpoints.description:
Returns the compact project records for all projects in the workspace.
*Note: This endpoint may timeout for large domains. Prefer the `/teams/{team_gid}/projects` endpoint.*
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.

endpoints.name: Create a project in a workspace
endpoints.description:
Returns the compact project records for all projects in the workspace.

If the workspace for your project is an organization, you must also
supply a team to share the project with.

Returns the full record of the newly created project.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.

endpoints.name: Remove a user from a workspace or organization
endpoints.description:
Remove a user from a workspace or organization.
The user making this call must be an admin in the workspace. The user can be referenced by their globally unique user ID or their email address.
Returns an empty data record.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.

endpoints.name: Get tags in a workspace
endpoints.description: Returns the compact tag records for some filtered set of tags. Use one or more of the parameters provided to filter the tags returned.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.

endpoints.name: Create a tag in a workspace
endpoints.description:
Creates a new tag in a workspace or organization.

Every tag is required to be created in a specific workspace or
organization, and this cannot be changed once set. Note that you can use
the workspace parameter regardless of whether or not it is an
organization.

Returns the full record of the newly created tag.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.

endpoints.name: Search tasks in a workspace
endpoints.description:
To mirror the functionality of the Asana web app's advanced search feature, the Asana API has a task search endpoint that allows you to build complex filters to find and retrieve the exact data you need.
#### Premium access
Like the Asana web product's advance search feature, this search endpoint will only be available to premium Asana users. A user is premium if any of the following is true:

- The workspace in which the search is being performed is a premium workspace - The user is a member of a premium team inside the workspace

Even if a user is only a member of a premium team inside a non-premium workspace, search will allow them to find data anywhere in the workspace, not just inside the premium team. Making a search request using credentials of a non-premium user will result in a `402 Payment Required` error.
#### Pagination
Search results are not stable; repeating the same query multiple times may return the data in a different order, even if the data do not change. Because of this, the traditional [pagination](https://developers.asana.com/docs/#pagination) available elsewhere in the Asana API is not available here. However, you can paginate manually by sorting the search results by their creation time and then modifying each subsequent query to exclude data you have already seen. Page sizes are limited to a maximum of 100 items, and can be specified by the `limit` query parameter.
#### Eventual consistency
Changes in Asana (regardless of whether they’re made though the web product or the API) are forwarded to our search infrastructure to be indexed. This process can take between 10 and 60 seconds to complete under normal operation, and longer during some production incidents. Making a change to a task that would alter its presence in a particular search query will not be reflected immediately. This is also true of the advanced search feature in the web product.
#### Rate limits
You may receive a `429 Too Many Requests` response if you hit any of our [rate limits](https://developers.asana.com/docs/#rate-limits).
#### Custom field parameters
| Parameter name | Custom field type | Accepted type |
|---|---|---|
| custom_fields.{gid}.is_set | All | Boolean |
| custom_fields.{gid}.value | Text | String |
| custom_fields.{gid}.value | Number | Number |
| custom_fields.{gid}.value | Enum | Enum option ID |
| custom_fields.{gid}.starts_with | Text only | String |
| custom_fields.{gid}.ends_with | Text only | String |
| custom_fields.{gid}.contains | Text only | String |
| custom_fields.{gid}.less_than | Number only | Number |
| custom_fields.{gid}.greater_than | Number only | Number |


For example, if the gid of the custom field is 12345, these query parameter to find tasks where it is set would be `custom_fields.12345.is_set=true`. To match an exact value for an enum custom field, use the gid of the desired enum option and not the name of the enum option: `custom_fields.12345.value=67890`.

**Not Supported**: searching for multiple exact matches of a custom field, searching for multi-enum custom field

*Note: If you specify `projects.any` and `sections.any`, you will receive tasks for the project **and** tasks for the section. If you're looking for only tasks in a section, omit the `projects.any` from the request.*
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.

endpoints.name: Get teams in a workspace
endpoints.description: Returns the compact records for all teams in the workspace visible to the authorized user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.

endpoints.name: Get objects via typeahead
endpoints.description:
Retrieves objects in the workspace based via an auto-completion/typeahead
search algorithm. This feature is meant to provide results quickly, so do
not rely on this API to provide extremely accurate search results. The
result set is limited to a single page of results with a maximum size, so
you won’t be able to fetch large numbers of results.

The typeahead search API provides search for objects from a single
workspace. This endpoint should be used to query for objects when
creating an auto-completion/typeahead search feature. This API is meant
to provide results quickly and should not be relied upon for accurate or
exhaustive search results. The results sets are limited in size and
cannot be paginated.

Queries return a compact representation of each object which is typically
the gid and name fields. Interested in a specific set of fields or all of
the fields?! Of course you are. Use field selectors to manipulate what
data is included in a response.

Resources with type `user` are returned in order of most contacted to
least contacted. This is determined by task assignments, adding the user
to projects, and adding the user as a follower to tasks, messages,
etc.

Resources with type `project` are returned in order of recency. This is
determined when the user visits the project, is added to the project, and
completes tasks in the project.

Resources with type `task` are returned with priority placed on tasks
the user is following, but no guarantee on the order of those tasks.

Resources with type `project_template` are returned with priority
placed on favorited project templates.

Leaving the `query` string empty or omitted will give you results, still
following the resource ordering above. This could be used to list users or
projects that are relevant for the requesting user's api token.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.

endpoints.name: Get users in a workspace or organization
endpoints.description:
Returns the compact records for all users in the specified workspace or organization.
Results are sorted alphabetically and limited to 2000. For more results use the `/users` endpoint.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.

endpoints.name: Get the workspace memberships for a workspace
endpoints.description: Returns the compact workspace membership records for the workspace.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ASANA_API_KEY}} with your ASANA API KEY. Replace {{WORKSPACE_GID}} with a real workspace_gid.
